ogTitle: Coomba Bay vs Coombadjha: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Coomba Bay and Coombadjha.
heading: Coomba Bay vs Coombadjha — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Coomba Bay, New South Wales and Coombadjha, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Coombadjha has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Coomba Bay (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Coomba Bay or Coombadjha safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Coomba Bay has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Coombadjha scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Coomba Bay scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Coombadjha scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Coomba Bay or Coombadjha?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Coombadjha has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
